states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection state before any command

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated; waiting for a transfer command

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for RNTO

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately after a successful or pending USER command.
Authentication (USER and PASS) must be completed before data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR can be executed.
A data connection configuration command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should be established before initiating data transfers.
The QUIT command can be used to terminate the session at any point after the initial connection.
The RNTO command must follow an RNFR command to complete a file rename operation.
If authentication fails (e.g., 530 response to PASS), the client may initiate a new authentication attempt with the USER command.
USER.username: Must follow the command name USER.
PASS.password: Must follow the command name PASS.
PORT.address: Format must be h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 representing IP and port.
TYPE.type_code: Commonly 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary.

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be sent immediately after a successful or pending USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP authentication sequence defined in RFC 959.

status_changes.description: Authentication (USER and PASS) must be completed before data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR can be executed.
status_changes.rationale: Fundamental security requirement for FTP.

status_changes.description: A data connection configuration command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should be established before initiating data transfers.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 requires a data connection setup before transfer commands can succeed.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command can be used to terminate the session at any point after the initial connection.
status_changes.rationale: Common implementation behavior for session management.

status_changes.description: The RNTO command must follow an RNFR command to complete a file rename operation.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 specifies this command sequence for renames.

status_changes.description: If authentication fails (e.g., 530 response to PASS), the client may initiate a new authentication attempt with the USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard recovery mechanism in FTP clients.

status_changes.description: USER.username: Must follow the command name USER.
status_changes.rationale: Syntactic requirement for the USER command.

status_changes.description: PASS.password: Must follow the command name PASS.
status_changes.rationale: Syntactic requirement for the PASS command.

status_changes.description: PORT.address: Format must be h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 representing IP and port.
status_changes.rationale: Specific address format defined in RFC 959.

status_changes.description: TYPE.type_code: Commonly 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary.
status_changes.rationale: Common type codes used in FTP.
